# Appendix B: Token Tracker Implementation

This appendix provides a reference implementation for a **token tracking system** that monitors context composition, enforces budgets, and triggers compression when thresholds are exceeded.

The design follows the token budget architecture described in Chapter 10.

## Token Estimation

Before tracking tokens, you need to estimate them. Exact tokenisation requires model-specific tokenisers, but a practical approximation works for budget management:

```javascript
// Approximate token estimation
// English text averages ~4 characters per token
// Code averages ~3.5 characters per token
// YAML/JSON averages ~3 characters per token
function estimateTokens(text, type = "text") {
  const charsPerToken = {
    text: 4,
    code: 3.5,
    yaml: 3,
    json: 3
  };

  const ratio = charsPerToken[type] ?? 4;
  return Math.ceil(text.length / ratio);
}

// For precise counting, use a tokeniser library
// import { encode } from "tiktoken";
// function countTokens(text) { return encode(text).length; }
```

## Token Tracker

The tracker maintains a running count of tokens by category and raises alerts when thresholds are crossed.

```javascript
class TokenTracker {
  constructor(config = {}) {
    this.budget = {
      constitutional: 0,
      skillCatalog: 0,
      activeSkills: 0,
      memory: 0,
      working: 0,
      buffer: 0
    };

    this.limits = {
      warn: config.warnAt ?? 12000,
      critical: config.criticalAt ?? 15000,
      hard: config.hardLimit ?? 20000
    };

    this.history = [];
    this.alerts = [];
  }

  // Update a category
  set(category, tokens) {
    if (!(category in this.budget)) {
      throw new Error(`Unknown budget category: ${category}`);
    }
    this.budget[category] = tokens;
    this.check();
  }

  // Increment a category
  add(category, tokens) {
    this.set(category, this.budget[category] + tokens);
  }

  // Get total usage
  get total() {
    return Object.values(this.budget).reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0);
  }

  // Get remaining budget
  get remaining() {
    return this.limits.warn - this.total;
  }

  // Get usage as percentage of warning threshold
  get usagePercent() {
    return Math.round((this.total / this.limits.warn) * 100);
  }

  // Check thresholds and raise alerts
  check() {
    const total = this.total;

    if (total > this.limits.hard) {
      this.alert("HARD_LIMIT", `Token count ${total} exceeds hard limit ${this.limits.hard}`);
      return "hard_limit";
    }

    if (total > this.limits.critical) {
      this.alert("CRITICAL", `Token count ${total} exceeds critical threshold ${this.limits.critical}`);
      return "critical";
    }

    if (total > this.limits.warn) {
      this.alert("WARNING", `Token count ${total} exceeds warning threshold ${this.limits.warn}`);
      return "warning";
    }

    return "ok";
  }

  alert(level, message) {
    const entry = {
      level,
      message,
      total: this.total,
      breakdown: { ...this.budget },
      timestamp: Date.now()
    };

    this.alerts.push(entry);
  }

  // Record a snapshot for history
  snapshot() {
    this.history.push({
      total: this.total,
      breakdown: { ...this.budget },
      timestamp: Date.now()
    });
  }

  // Get a formatted breakdown
  report() {
    const lines = [];
    lines.push(`Token Budget Report (${this.usagePercent}% of warning threshold)`);
    lines.push("─".repeat(50));

    for (const [category, tokens] of Object.entries(this.budget)) {
      const bar = "█".repeat(Math.round(tokens / 200));
      const pct = Math.round((tokens / this.total) * 100);
      lines.push(`  ${category.padEnd(16)} ${String(tokens).padStart(6)} (${pct}%) ${bar}`);
    }

    lines.push("─".repeat(50));
    lines.push(`  ${"TOTAL".padEnd(16)} ${String(this.total).padStart(6)}`);
    lines.push(`  ${"Remaining".padEnd(16)} ${String(this.remaining).padStart(6)}`);

    return lines.join("\n");
  }
}
```

## Budget Allocation Presets

Pre-defined allocations for common scenarios:

```javascript
const BUDGET_PRESETS = {
  // Standard: balanced allocation for general use
  standard: {
    constitutional: 2000,
    skillCatalog: 100,
    activeSkills: 5000,
    memory: 2000,
    working: 2000,
    buffer: 900
  },

  // Skill-heavy: more room for active skills
  skillHeavy: {
    constitutional: 2000,
    skillCatalog: 200,
    activeSkills: 7000,
    memory: 1500,
    working: 1500,
    buffer: 800
  },

  // Memory-heavy: more room for conversation history
  memoryHeavy: {
    constitutional: 2000,
    skillCatalog: 100,
    activeSkills: 3000,
    memory: 4000,
    working: 2000,
    buffer: 900
  },

  // Minimal: tight budget for smaller models
  minimal: {
    constitutional: 1500,
    skillCatalog: 100,
    activeSkills: 3000,
    memory: 1000,
    working: 1000,
    buffer: 400
  }
};

function createTrackerWithPreset(preset) {
  const alloc = BUDGET_PRESETS[preset];
  if (!alloc) throw new Error(`Unknown preset: ${preset}`);

  const tracker = new TokenTracker({
    warnAt: Object.values(alloc).reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0),
    criticalAt: Math.round(Object.values(alloc).reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) * 1.25),
    hardLimit: Math.round(Object.values(alloc).reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) * 1.5)
  });

  return tracker;
}
```

## Compression Trigger

When the tracker raises an alert, the compression pipeline activates:

```javascript
function onBudgetAlert(tracker, context) {
  const status = tracker.check();

  if (status === "warning") {
    // Soft compression: summarise old memory, drop optional skills
    context.memory = summariseOldTurns(context.memory, keepLast: 5);
    tracker.set("memory", estimateTokens(context.memory));
  }

  if (status === "critical") {
    // Hard compression: drop all non-core skills, aggressive summarisation
    context.activeSkills = context.activeSkills.filter(s => s.tier === "core");
    context.memory = summariseOldTurns(context.memory, keepLast: 2);

    tracker.set("activeSkills", context.activeSkills.reduce(
      (sum, s) => sum + estimateTokens(s.content), 0
    ));
    tracker.set("memory", estimateTokens(context.memory));
  }

  if (status === "hard_limit") {
    // Emergency: constitutional only
    context.activeSkills = [];
    context.memory = extractKeyFacts(context.memory);
    context.working = truncateToEssentials(context.working);

    tracker.set("activeSkills", 0);
    tracker.set("memory", estimateTokens(context.memory));
    tracker.set("working", estimateTokens(context.working));
  }
}
```

## Usage Example

```javascript
const tracker = createTrackerWithPreset("standard");

// Set constitutional (never changes)
tracker.set("constitutional", 2000);
tracker.set("skillCatalog", 250);

// As skills activate
tracker.set("activeSkills", 3500);

// As conversation grows
tracker.set("memory", 1800);
tracker.set("working", 1200);

// Check status
console.log(tracker.report());
// Token Budget Report (73% of warning threshold)
// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
//   constitutional    2000 (23%) ██████████
//   skillCatalog       250 ( 3%) █
//   activeSkills      3500 (40%) █████████████████
//   memory            1800 (21%) █████████
//   working           1200 (14%) ██████
//   buffer               0 ( 0%)
// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
//   TOTAL             8750
//   Remaining         3250

// Take a snapshot for history
tracker.snapshot();
```

## Key Sources

- Chapter 10: Token Budget Architecture (12,000 token rule, allocation breakdown)
- Chapter 11: Compression Strategies (priority order: dedupe, summarise, truncate, drop)
- Chapter 12: Skill Architecture (skill activation/deactivation lifecycle)
